description: many-objective optimization with NSGA-III using reference directions on a 4+ objective benchmark (read-only)
rules: read-only — computes a high-dimensional front and writes a report; mutates nothing remote or live
runs the vendored many_objective_example.py core (NSGA-III on DTLZ2, das-dennis reference directions) via python3
forces MPLBACKEND=Agg so the Parallel Coordinate Plot never blocks
captures stdout (ref-dir count, solution count, per-objective min/max) to record_store/many_objective.txt
requires the pymoo package at run time; degrades to an empty {} report if it is absent
usage: Set PROBLEM (e.g. dtlz2), N_OBJ (4+), N_PARTITIONS (reference-direction partitions), N_GEN. Output: many_objective.txt.
limitation: The vendored core runs its built-in 5-objective benchmark (DTLZ2, NSGA-III); vars document intent. Needs the pymoo library installed to produce real results.
